---
title: "Who Will Win the 2026 World Cup?"
subtitle: "A Data-Driven Prediction"
author: "Merna Saad | UCSD Rady School of Management"
date: "April 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    center: true
    width: 1200
    height: 700
embed-resources: true
---

## The Tournament

The 2026 FIFA World Cup is being hosted across the **United States, Canada, and Mexico**, making it the first ever 48-team World Cup.

More teams. More games. More unpredictability.

The question on everyone's mind: **who's lifting the trophy?**

## The Contenders {.smaller}

Based on historical performance, FIFA rankings, squad depth, and recent form:

| Rank | Team | Key Strength | Titles |
|------|------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Argentina | Defending champions, winning mentality | 3 |
| 2 | Brazil | Most titles ever, home continent advantage | 5 |
| 3 | France | Back-to-back finalist, deep squad | 2 |
| 4 | Spain | Euro 2024 champions, young and hungry | 1 |
| 5 | England | Strong Premier League core, consistently improving | 1 |

The data points to South America. But football is never that simple.

## My Prediction: Argentina

After running classification models on historical match data, team rankings, and player statistics:

- Defending champions with proven tournament DNA
- Messi's final World Cup creates unmatched motivation
- Best squad chemistry of any national team right now
- Massive fanbase on American soil

[*Vamos Argentina!*]{style="color: #b5704f; font-size: 1.2em;"}
